description: Authoritative pure-data blocks extracted from AUDIT.md per E004. This file is the normative source of truth for the 11 closed-set / ordered-list / regex-pattern schemas that AUDIT.md's rules constrain. AUDIT.md retains explanatory prose at each original location and points here for the actual values. F007 (schema-contract-stability fixture) validates each block's structural invariants + cross-block consistency against this file.

type3_mapping._comment: §1 'The 12 canonical TYPE3 codes'. Every item ID is AUD-YYYY-MM-DD-<TYPE3>-<NNNN>. The 12 three-letter codes below are the COMPLETE closed set — no synonyms, no invention. Forbidden codes (always fail conformance): QA→QLT, DX→DEV, DOCS→DOC, PERF→PRF, ARCH→ARC. TYPE3 must be exactly 3 characters.

status_state_machine._comment: §STATUS STATE MACHINE. 7-element closed set of status names + allowed transitions. Terminal statuses (DONE, WONT_DO, REJECTED) appear in state but have no outgoing edges. Every transition appends to history[], transitions.jsonl, and CHANGELOG.md. Only APPROVED+AGENT is execute-eligible.

moscow_priorities._comment: §Definitions §MoSCoW. Ordered (priority-descending) closed set of 4 values. MUST blocks release; WONT terminalizes to wont-do.json.

semantics.MUST: Security-critical, data loss, broken builds, compliance gaps. Block release.
semantics.SHOULD: High-value perf / reliability / maintainability. Address this cycle.
semantics.WONT: Out of scope or explicitly declined. Terminalize to wont-do.json.

scanner_manifest._comment: §Scanner manifest (MANDATORY in generated_runs[N].scanners). 8-element ordered closed set. Consolidate mode MAY emit ["consolidate"] instead. Any other subset is non-conformant.

evidence_redaction_patterns._comment: §EVIDENCE REDACTION. 8 regex→label pairs applied to every evidence[].snippet and finding text BEFORE any persistence. Patterns target secret VALUES, not structural keywords (YAML key names, shell var refs, function params, schema keywords). Extension seam: config.yaml → redaction.extra_patterns.

details_schema_by_level._comment: §ITEM SCHEMA 'details schema by level'. The details object schema VARIES BY LEVEL. Over-filling is a hard violation equal in severity to under-filling. Set equality: set(it.details.keys()) == REQUIRED ∪ (allowed_subset_used) AND keys ∩ FORBIDDEN == ∅.

counts_closed_sets._comment: §OUTPUT CONTRACT 'Derivation rule'. counts.* are state snapshots (totals over live items[], cumulative across all runs). Closed-set counts.* keys: EXACTLY total, by_level, by_moscow, by_assignee, by_status. Flow metrics (new/merged/deduped) live on generated_runs[N], NOT counts. Emit every key even when zero.

severity_ladder._comment: §Severity (security & performance scanners). 5-level ordered ladder from most-severe to least-severe. Used by security + performance scanners; other scanners may use a subset.

perf_thresholds._comment: §Hot path and §Large bundle. Numeric thresholds defining the 'hot path' and 'large bundle' scanner triggers. Values drive the performance scanner's heuristics.

sort_order._comment: §Sort Order (canonical). 4-key sort applied recursively at every level of the hierarchy. Items are written on disk in sorted pre-order — readers never re-sort. Cross-artifact ordering invariant: index.json, daily .md, and mirror .json must have IDENTICAL orderings.

fivew_oneh_twoc_fivem._comment: §5W1H2C5M. Every task populates every field with grounded, finding-specific analysis. Stories and epics summarize from their tasks. FORBIDDEN filler patterns (each a hard violation) are preserved in AUDIT.md prose — this block captures only the sub-block roster.

semantics.Cost: effort hours, risk level, blast radius
semantics.Constraints: what cannot change; deps; deadlines; back-compat requirements

bootstrap_readme_sections._comment: §Bootstrap README. Ordered spec for `.audit/README.md` (the human-facing guide emitted on bootstrap and regenerated when missing). This block captures the ORDERED section roster + regeneration rules. The README's literal text is templated from `templates/CHEAT-SHEET.md.tmpl` and `templates/TROUBLESHOOTING.md.tmpl` for §7 and §8; sections 1-6 and 9 are prose derived from this spec. D04 extracted (2026-04-21) from AUDIT.md §Bootstrap README.

sections.name: Title + orientation paragraph
sections.required_content: # Audit System heading; one-paragraph orientation stating (a) the directory is agent-managed, (b) daily scans land in reports/<date>.md, (c) nothing else in the repo is modified without explicit approval.

sections.name: The loop (ASCII diagram)
sections.required_content: SCAN → PROPOSED → APPROVED → IN_PROGRESS → DONE main path; DEFERRED and REJECTED branches; terminal WONT_DO. Sourced from SCHEMA.json § status_state_machine.

sections.name: Daily 3-step
sections.required_content: Open today's report; edit `status:` to APPROVED / WONT_DO / DEFERRED; run execute mode.

sections.name: Status reference
sections.required_content: The 7 status values from SCHEMA.json § status_state_machine.statuses, each with a one-line meaning.

sections.name: MoSCoW reference
sections.required_content: The 4 priority levels from SCHEMA.json § moscow_priorities.levels with one-line meanings.

sections.name: Cheat sheet
sections.required_content: Verbatim contents of `templates/CHEAT-SHEET.md.tmpl` under a `## Cheat sheet` heading.

sections.name: Troubleshooting
sections.required_content: Verbatim contents of `templates/TROUBLESHOOTING.md.tmpl` under a `## Troubleshooting` heading.

regeneration_rules.on_bootstrap: Always write. Step 0 emits `.audit/README.md` as part of the scaffold.
regeneration_rules.on_scan_if_missing: Regenerate silently in Step 2 and log `readme_regenerated` note in the Run Log.
regeneration_rules.never_overwrite_existing: A normal scan MUST NOT overwrite an existing README. Forced refresh requires `MODE=consolidate`.
